top.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
top.summary: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

top.title: Meta and TikTok let harmful content rise after evidence outrage drove engagement, say whistleblowers
top.summary: Companies allowed more harmful content on user’s feeds, knowing their algorithms ran on outrage, BBC hears.

top.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
top.summary: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

top.title: Firms urged to check if other users edited their data on Companies House
top.summary: The glitch meant logged-in users could view and edit other companies' details without their consent.

top.title: Is this product 'human-made'? The race to establish an AI-free logo
top.summary: The backlash to the growing use of the tech has led to an explosion in attempts to come up with 'AI-Free' logo that could be used globally.

top.title: She spent 16 hours on Instagram in a day. It's up to a jury to decide if Meta is to blame
top.summary: A landmark lawsuit will set the stage for thousands of people who say social media platforms are intentionally addictive.
